summary: This case centers on persistent, low-to-moderate grief after a breakup with maladaptive social-media checking that triggers immediate somatic distress and negative self-evaluation. The proposed modifiable levers span social-environmental boundaries (to curb checking), cognitive and affective regulation (reappraisal, self-compassion, mindfulness), behavioral activation and social connectedness, sleep and physiological regulation, and identity/meaning work to rebuild self-worth independent of the ex. Together, these targets aim to reduce C02 (checking), C04 (somatic distress), C03 (self-criticism/shame), C06 (rumination/ Social comparison), and C01 (persistent grief), while providing concrete measurement and feasible rollout timelines.

solutions.predictor_variable: Digital Boundaries for Social Media Checking (ex unfollow/mute/block; scheduled daily check window; disable non-essential notifications)
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a structured digital boundary protocol: unfollow/mute/exclude the ex’s profile; turn off ex-related notifications; schedule a single 10-minute daily window for checking (if at all); use reminders to resist outside-window checking. Maintain a log of checks.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces cue-induced urges and negative reinforcement from checking by altering the environment and limiting exposure to triggering content, thereby decreasing subsequent distress and self-criticism.
solutions.measurement: Objective: days with zero ex-profile checks; self-reported checks/day; distress level after checking (0–10).
solutions.time_horizon: 2–6 weeks to establish habit; re-evaluate at 8 weeks.

solutions.solution_candidate: Weekly 20-minute CBT-based reframing plus daily 5-minute self-compassion micropractice (guided). Target self-criticism (C03) and distress after checking (C04).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improves interpretation of social-media cues, reduces shame, and fosters self-kindness, dampening emotional reactivity and the urge to engage in avoidance behaviors.
solutions.measurement: Self-criticism/shame ratings after checking (0–10); self-compassion ratings (0–10); frequency of automatic negative thoughts.

solutions.solution_candidate: 10-minute daily mindfulness practice (breath/body scan) plus brief rumination-focused acceptance exercise (guided audio).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances non-judgmental awareness of urges to check, reduces cognitive reactivity and physiological arousal after checking, and lowers rumination.
solutions.measurement: State rumination scores (0–10); distress after checking (0–10); checks per day.

solutions.solution_candidate: Schedule one meaningful activity daily (walk, exercise, hobby) and two social activities weekly; track mood before/after activities.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increases positive affect and engagement, reduces passive rumination and excessive social comparison, and improves baseline mood to buffer grief.
solutions.measurement: Activity log entries per day; mood ratings pre/post activity (0–10); days with at least one activity.

solutions.solution_candidate: Establish consistent sleep/wake times, reduce evening screens, wind-down routine, monitor sleep duration/quality; optimize light exposure.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Better sleep supports emotion regulation and cognitive control, reducing sensitivity to social-media cues and somatic distress.
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration; sleep quality rating (0–10); wake after sleep onset; daily mood.

solutions.solution_candidate: Identify 2–3 trusted friends/family; schedule weekly check-ins; join a supportive group; share distress coping strategies; reduce isolation.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social connectedness buffers grief and decreases reliance on ex-related cues; provides corrective feedback to self-evaluation and mitigates rumination.
solutions.measurement: Number of supportive contacts per week; belonging scale scores; mood after social interaction (0–10).

solutions.solution_candidate: Daily self-compassion practice; values identification; journaling to rebuild identity outside the ex; weekly reflection on strengths.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Strengthens self-worth independent of the relationship and reduces shame, thus lowering vulnerability to distress after checking.
solutions.measurement: Self-esteem (Rosenberg) weekly; self-compassion scale after practice; daily diaries.

solutions.solution_candidate: Values clarification and daily meaning-focused journaling; identify near-term goals; implement small steps toward personal life goals.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Shifts attention from longing to pursuing personal meaning, reduces rumination about the ex, and promotes forward movement.
solutions.measurement: Meaning/Purpose scale; daily goal progress logs; mood tracking.
